response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist software engineer with roughly 20 years of experience across web, mobile, embedded, and platform work, and holds a relevant CS degree. Their current role at re:cinq (April 2025–present) does involve AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, which is directly relevant to the role's agentic workflow focus. However, that exposure is approximately one year old, far short of the required 8+ years of production-grade AI delivery. The resume shows no Python proficiency, no mention of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks such as LangGraph or CrewAI. Cloud experience is limited to AWS CDK for a pricing service rather than AI-centric infrastructure. The candidate would likely fit a mid-level generalist or AI-adjacent role, but does not yet demonstrate the depth of AI-specific production experience this staff-level position demands.
strengths: Active hands-on involvement with Model Context Protocol (MCP) code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure at re:cinq, directly aligning with the role's agentic systems focus
Strong TypeScript/Node.js background across multiple production companies, satisfying the language requirement and demonstrating sustained engineering quality
Proven polyglot and platform breadth (web, mobile, embedded, DevOps, cloud) indicating fast ramp-up capability and systems-level thinking
concerns: No Python mentioned anywhere in the resume — the primary language for AI/ML pipelines, SDKs, and frameworks required by this role
Zero documented experience with RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation/observability, or multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, etc.)
AI-specific work is limited to ~13 months at the current role; the position requires a minimum of 8 years shipping production-grade AI solutions, leaving a gap of roughly 7 years
